sentence_reviews.text: An autoencoder compresses an input into a latent code and reconstructs it, learning representations through an information bottleneck rather than explicit labels.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited book directly treats autoencoders as encoder-decoder models trained to reconstruct inputs and learn representations without labels; bottleneck wording is consistent with undercomplete/regularized autoencoders.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Affine transforms & dense layers, Reconstruction objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Autoencoders is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning Chapter 6 feedforward networks and Chapter 14 autoencoders.
sentence_reviews.notes: Standard autoencoders are often built from affine/dense layers, though convolutional and other variants exist.

sentence_reviews.text: Autoencoders consumes, emits, or organizes information using Latent variables & hidden state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning Chapter 14 representations/codes; PML latent-variable modeling topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Autoencoder codes are latent representations; 'latent variables' is defensible but more probabilistic than all autoencoder variants require.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Autoencoders is modeled as depending on Affine transforms & dense layers, Reconstruction objectives and feeding downstream nodes such as Variational autoencoders, VQ-VAE & learned discrete tokenizers, Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Autoencoders has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Reconstruction objectives because Reconstruction objectives is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.

edge_reviews.notes: Dense affine components are a common implementation basis for autoencoders, but not mandatory for every autoencoder architecture.
The encoder code is a latent representation; common strength is appropriate because not all autoencoders are explicitly probabilistic latent-variable models.

edge_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical dependency edge with relationship type, strength, direction, and rationale.
